verses.text: Jesus shows Himself at the sea; Peter goes fishing and that night catches nothing. Empty nets. Without His word their labour fails.
Jesus shows Himself at the sea; Peter goes fishing and that night catches nothing. Empty nets. Without His word their labour fails.
Jesus shows Himself at the sea; Peter goes fishing and that night catches nothing. Empty nets. Without His word their labour fails.
'Cast the net on the right side'; they catch a multitude they cannot draw. Obedient catch. At His word the nets are filled.
'Cast the net on the right side'; they catch a multitude they cannot draw. Obedient catch. At His word the nets are filled.
'Cast the net on the right side'; they catch a multitude they cannot draw. Obedient catch. At His word the nets are filled.
'It is the Lord'; Peter casts himself into the sea. Eager love. Peter cannot wait to come to his Lord.
'It is the Lord'; Peter casts himself into the sea. Eager love. Peter cannot wait to come to his Lord.
They find fish and bread on a fire; the net holds a hundred fifty-three, yet unbroken. Bountiful provision. The risen Lord feeds His own.
They find fish and bread on a fire; the net holds a hundred fifty-three, yet unbroken. Bountiful provision. The risen Lord feeds His own.
They find fish and bread on a fire; the net holds a hundred fifty-three, yet unbroken. Bountiful provision. The risen Lord feeds His own.
'Come and dine'; this was the third time He showed Himself after rising. Quiet fellowship. He renews communion with His disciples.
'Come and dine'; this was the third time He showed Himself after rising. Quiet fellowship. He renews communion with His disciples.
'Come and dine'; this was the third time He showed Himself after rising. Quiet fellowship. He renews communion with His disciples.
'Simon... lovest thou me? ... Feed my lambs'; again, 'Feed my sheep.' Restored and charged. The thrice-denying Peter is thrice owned and commissioned.
'Simon... lovest thou me? ... Feed my lambs'; again, 'Feed my sheep.' Restored and charged. The thrice-denying Peter is thrice owned and commissioned.
A third time, 'Lovest thou me? ... Feed my sheep'; He foretells Peter's death; 'Follow me.' Loving and following. Love for Christ is proved in feeding His flock.
A third time, 'Lovest thou me? ... Feed my sheep'; He foretells Peter's death; 'Follow me.' Loving and following. Love for Christ is proved in feeding His flock.
A third time, 'Lovest thou me? ... Feed my sheep'; He foretells Peter's death; 'Follow me.' Loving and following. Love for Christ is proved in feeding His flock.
Peter asks of John; 'if I will that he tarry... what is that to thee? follow thou me.' Mind thy calling. Each must follow Christ in his own appointed way.
Peter asks of John; 'if I will that he tarry... what is that to thee? follow thou me.' Mind thy calling. Each must follow Christ in his own appointed way.
Peter asks of John; 'if I will that he tarry... what is that to thee? follow thou me.' Mind thy calling. Each must follow Christ in his own appointed way.
Peter asks of John; 'if I will that he tarry... what is that to thee? follow thou me.' Mind thy calling. Each must follow Christ in his own appointed way.
This is the disciple that wrote these things; 'the world... could not contain the books.' Boundless deeds. The record is true, yet only a part of all He did.
This is the disciple that wrote these things; 'the world... could not contain the books.' Boundless deeds. The record is true, yet only a part of all He did.
